def: s.f. Deliberation, &c. (i.q. vićāraṇ); — the Mīmānsā system. of philosophy.
part. Deliberating, considering, reflecting, thinking, judging, &c.
adj. & s.m. ( = bićārū, or bićārī) = vićārak, or bićārak, qq.v. s.m. = bićārak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Deliberating; investigating; discussing; judging; — investigator, discriminator, inspector, judge.
part. Deliberated about, considered, reflected on; examined, inquired into, discussed, investigated, judged; decided, determined.

ety: wićār˚ = Prk. विचार(इ) or विचारे(इ) = S. विचारय(ति), caus. of वि+चर्
def: v.t. To think, consider, &c. (see bićārnā, the pop. form).
adj. & s.m. ( = bićārū, or bićārī) = vićārak, or bićārak, qq.v. s.m. = bićārak, q.v. adj. & s.m. Deliberating; investigating; discussing; judging; — investigator, discriminator, inspector, judge.
part. adj. (f. -ā), To be deliberated about, to be weighed or discussed, fit or proper for consideration, &c.; — questionable, doubtful.
s.m. = S وچهردك विच्छर्दक viććhardak, s.m. = S وچهندك विच्छन्दक viććhandak, s.m. A building consisting of several stories and surrounded by a portico; a large building; a palace; a temple.
s.m. = S وچهردك विच्छर्दक viććhardak, s.m. = S وچهند विच्छन्द viććhand, s.m. A building consisting of several stories and surrounded by a portico; a large building; a palace; a temple.
s.m. = S وچهند विच्छन्द viććhand, s.m. = S وچهندك विच्छन्दक viććhandak, s.m. A building consisting of several stories and surrounded by a portico; a large building; a palace; a temple.

s.m. Cutting asunder, dividing, breaking, cutting off; separation; disjunction; interruption; dispersion; distribution; termination; prohibition, prevention; removal; — dissension, difference; space, interval; — division (of a book), section, chapter.
adj. Small, little.
s.f. Doubt, uncertainty; error, mistake.

adj. Variegated, diversified; speckled, spotted, party-coloured; various, varied, diverse, motley; — painted, coloured, ornamented; — handsome, beautiful, pleasing; admirable, wonderful, surprising; rare.
s.f. = بد bid, q.v. s.f. A small hole or pit into which children in play endeavour to throw balls or marbles.
s.f. Knowledge, learning, wisdom; understanding, intellect.
s.f. = بدا bidā, q.v. s.f. Permission to depart, dismission, taking leave; departure; farewell
subs: (-ko), To dismiss, permit (one) to depart; to bid farewell; to see (one) off
v.n. To take leave, say farewell, depart; to depart this life, to die.

ety: v.n. fr. ودع 'to leave at liberty, to permit,' &c.
def: s.m. Adieu, farewell; parting; bidding farewell
subs: (-ko), To bid adieu or farewell (to), to dismiss.

def: s.m. (f. -ā), A knower; a wise man, a sage.

part. adj. (f. -ā), Burnt; inflamed; — well-bred; clever, shrewd, knowing, learned; able, skilled; witty, sharp; — subtle, cunning, sly, crafty, artful, intriguing; — s.m. A learned or clever man, a scholar, a Pandit; — an artful or a crafty person; an intriguer; — a libertine, lecher.
s.f. = S ودگدهتو विदग्धत्व vi-dagdhatva, s.m. Sharpness, shrewdness, cleverness, learnedness, sagacity, wit, &c.
s.m. = S ودگدهتا विदग्धता vi-dagdhatā, s.f. Sharpness, shrewdness, cleverness, learnedness, sagacity, wit, &c.
s.f. = بدائي bidāʼī, q.v. s.f. Presents made by a host to a visitor, or to a servant, on dismissing him; dismissal; departure ( = bidā).
adj. Bidding farewell, valedictory.
s.m. A cat (i.q. bilār); — the eye-ball.
s.m. A cat; — application of ointment to the exterior of the eye (esp. to the eye-lids when the lashes have fallen off or are ulcerated); — yellow orpiment.
s.m. Imitation; — afflicting, distressing, annoyance.

s.m. Imitation, copying, assuming a similar appearance or dress; disguise, masquerade; — supernatural assumption of a borrowed form; transformation; imposture; — deceiving, deception, fraud; breach of faith; — disappointing, frustrating; — afflicting, distressing; vexation, mortification; pain, distress, misery; — ridiculing; ridiculousness, ludicrousness.

s.f. = viḍamban, q.v. s.m. Imitation, copying, assuming a similar appearance or dress; disguise, masquerade; — supernatural assumption of a borrowed form; transformation; imposture; — deceiving, deception, fraud; breach of faith; — disappointing, frustrating; — afflicting, distressing; vexation, mortification; pain, distress, misery; — ridiculing; ridiculousness, ludicrousness.

part. (f. -ā), Imitated, copied; assumed, put on, simulated; transformed; distorted; — mocked at, ridiculed, derided; — deceived, disappointed, frustrated; — vexed, mortified; — afflicted, distressed; low, abject, poor, miserable.
adj. Tearing asunder, rending; — s.m. The Indian prickly pear, Cactus indicus.
s.m. Tearing, rending, &c. (see bidāraṇ); — paining, afflicting; — killing, massacre, slaughter; — war, battle.
s.m. Name of a district and city to the south-west of Bengal (the modern Berar proper); — the king of Vidarbh; — any dry or desert soil.

ety: widār˚ = Prk. विदार(इ), or विदारे(इ) = S. विदारय(ति), rt. वि+दॄ
def: v.t. To tear, rend, &c. (i.q. bidārnā, q.v.). v.t. To tear, rend, &c. (see bidāran).
v.n. To be torn or rent asunder; to burst open, to burst, split, go asunder.
adj. (f. -ā), Without body, bodiless, incorporeal; trunkless; — s.m. A sovereign of the family of Janak, king of Mithilā.
s.f. A district in the province of Bihār (the ancient Mithilā, or the modern Tirhut); — (S. vi-dehās), s.m. pl. The people of Videha.
s.m. Another country, foreign country, &c. (see bides)
subs: vulg. videshaj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Born or produced in a foreign country; foreign, exotic; a foreigner; an exotic
vulg. videśag, or videś-gāmī, adj. & s.m. Going abroad or to a foreign country; — one who goes abroad; a traveller
s.m. Going abroad, travelling in foreign lands.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), See s.v. videś. s.m. Another country, foreign country, &c. (see bides)
subs: vulg. videshaj, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Born or produced in a foreign country; foreign, exotic; a foreigner; an exotic
vulg. videśag, or videś-gāmī, adj. & s.m. Going abroad or to a foreign country; — one who goes abroad; a traveller
s.m. Going abroad, travelling in foreign lands.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), See the pop. form bidesī.
s.f. contrac. of vidhā, q.v.
s.f. Form, formula, rule; — manner, kind, sort; — act, action; — doing well, thriving, prosperity, affluence; — wages, hire; — fodder, food (of horses, elephants, &c.).
s.f. Piercing, penetrating, penetration.
s.m. Form, manner, &c. (i.q. vidhā); fold (frequently used in Bahu-vrīhi compounds, e.g. dvi-vidh).

s.m. Sending; ordering; arrangement, &c. (i.q. bidhān, q.v.); — form, manner, mode; — act, action; the performance of an act prescribed by religion or law; — means, expedient; — wealth; — fodder (of elephants).
adj. (f. -ā), Devoid of wealth, indigent, poor; — s.m. An indigent per son, a poor man
s.m. An epithet of Brahmā.
s.f. = S ودهنتو विधनत्व vi-dhanatva, s.m. Destitution, indigence, poverty.
s.m. = S ودهنتا विधनता vi-dhanatā, s.f. Destitution, indigence, poverty.
s.m. A different religion (from one's own); heterodoxy; apostasy.
s.m. Arranger, disposer, &c. (i.q. bidhātā, q.v.); — an epithet of Kām-dev.
s.f. A widow (i.q. bidhwā)
s.m. = bidhawā, and vidhātā, qq.v. s.f. Widow.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Placing, fixing, securing; arranging, disposing; making, performing, causing, occasioning; establishing a rule or law, directing, prescribing, enjoining; — consigning, delivering; — one who causes (a thing) to be fixed or secure, &c.; — one who entrusts or deposits (anything).

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī) = vi-dhāyak, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Placing, fixing, securing; arranging, disposing; making, performing, causing, occasioning; establishing a rule or law, directing, prescribing, enjoining; — consigning, delivering; — one who causes (a thing) to be fixed or secure, &c.; — one who entrusts or deposits (anything).

part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), To be arranged or prescribed; to be enjoined (as a rule, &c.); to be performed, or practised, or done; liable to be ruled or governed or influenced (by), governable, subject (to); — compliant, submissive, tractable, manageable; — (in Gram.) the predicate.

adj. corr. of ودهيه vidheya, q.v. part. adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), To be arranged or prescribed; to be enjoined (as a rule, &c.); to be performed, or practised, or done; liable to be ruled or governed or influenced (by), governable, subject (to); — compliant, submissive, tractable, manageable; — (in Gram.) the predicate.

s.f. = S ودهيتو विधेयत्व vi-dheyatva, s.m. Fitness to be prescribed or enjoined; fitness for enactment (as a rule or law); — necessary or proper act or conduct; practicability; — tractableness, docility, submission.

s.m. = S ودهيتا विधेयता vi-dheyatā, s.f. Fitness to be prescribed or enjoined; fitness for enactment (as a rule or law); — necessary or proper act or conduct; practicability; — tractableness, docility, submission.
s.m. A rule, form, formula, &c. (see bidhi or bidh)
subs: s.m. An epithet of Brahmalok; — the sacred feet of Brahma
adv. After due observance of rule; according to rule, agreeably to prescribed law

vulg. bidhigya, adj. 'Rule-knowing,' acquainted with the laws or precepts, learned in ritual; knowing how to proceed; — s.m. One who knows the prescribed mode or form; a well-informed person; a Brāhman who knows the ritual
s.m. A priest whose business it is to see that everything at a sacrifice is done according to prescribed rules and to correct any deviation from them
vidhi-vat, or vidhi-vant, s.m. (f. -vatī), One who acts in conformity with prescribed rules
adv. Agreeably to rule, according to law, in due form, conformably to established ordinances; regularly, duly
vulg. vidh-vyavahār, s.m. The conduct or the practices of Brahmā
adj. Consisting of Brahmā, Vishṇu, and Śiva; possessed of, or imbued with, the divine. (For other compounds, see bidhi.)

def: s.m. The moon; — a name of Brahmā (lit. 'one who arranges or disposes or rules or maintains'; a name of Vishṇu; — a Rākshas, a fiend (see bidhu or bidh); — an expiatory oblation; — camphor
subs: s.m. 'Moon-troubler,' an epithet of Rāhu or the personified ascending Node (causing the moon's eclipses)
adj. (f. -ī), Moon-faced; having a moon-like or beautiful face (syn. māh-rūʼe)

def: s.m. See s.v. ودهو vidhu. s.m. The moon; — a name of Brahmā (lit. 'one who arranges or disposes or rules or maintains'; a name of Vishṇu; — a Rākshas, a fiend (see bidhu or bidh); — an expiatory oblation; — camphor
subs: s.m. 'Moon-troubler,' an epithet of Rāhu or the personified ascending Node (causing the moon's eclipses)
adj. (f. -ī), Moon-faced; having a moon-like or beautiful face (syn. māh-rūʼe)

def: adj. (f. -ā), Agitated, troubled, distressed, confused, trembling; suffering separation or absence from a mistress or lover; being in a state of bereavement; abandoned, deserted; — adverse, hostile; — s.m. One who is agitated, &c.; one who is suffering separation or absence from a beloved object; — a widower; — (S. vidhuram), s.m. Agitation of mind, distress, confusion, anxiety, alarm, terror; danger; separation (from a mistress or lover).
s.m. Falling to pieces, ruin, destruction, &c. (i.q. bidhwaṇs, q.v.); — aversion, dislike, enmity, disrespect; — insult, offence.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Falling asunder, being ruined, perishing; — causing to fall, overthrowing, ruining, destroying; — hostile, adverse; — overthrower, destroyer, &c.
s.m. corr. of vindhyā-ćakr, q.v.
s.m. corr. of vindhya-kshetr, q.v.
s.f. = vidiś, q.v. s.f. An intermediate point of the compass (as N.E., S.E., S.W., or N.W.).
part. (f. -ā), Rent asunder, burst, open, broken open, torn, split, ripped up; — expanded, opened.
s.f. An intermediate point of the compass (as N.E., S.E., S.W., or N.W.).
s.f. 1˚ = vidiś, q.v.; — 2˚ name of the capital of the district of Daśārṇā; — name of a river in Mālwā.

part. (f. -ā), Perceived, understood, known; familiar; — famed, famous, renowned; — apprized, informed; — represented; promised; agreed; — s.m. A well-informed man, a learned man, a sage; — (S. viditam), s.m. Knowledge, information; — representation.
s.m. A tree bearing precious gems; — coral, a coral-tree; — a young sprout or shoot.
adj. (f. -ā), Knowing, wise, learned, intelligent; clever, skilful; intriguing; — s.m. A learned or clever man; — an intriguer; — name of the younger brother of Dhrita-rāshṭra and Pāṇḍu.
adj. Very far, distant, remote; — s.m. Name of a city and mountain whence the lapis lazuli is brought
subs: vulg. vidūraj, s.m. The lapis lazuli (lit. 'produced in Vidūr').

def: s.m. See s.v. vidūr. adj. (f. -ā), Knowing, wise, learned, intelligent; clever, skilful; intriguing; — s.m. A learned or clever man; — an intriguer; — name of the younger brother of Dhrita-rāshṭra and Pāṇḍu.

s.m. corr. of vidūraj, q.v. s.m. See s.v. vidūr. adj. (f. -ā), Knowing, wise, learned, intelligent; clever, skilful; intriguing; — s.m. A learned or clever man; — an intriguer; — name of the younger brother of Dhrita-rāshṭra and Pāṇḍu.
s.m. Name of a king (a son of Su-rath); — name of a son of Bhajamān.

adj. & s.m. = vidur, q.v. adj. (f. -ā), Knowing, wise, learned, intelligent; clever, skilful; intriguing; — s.m. A learned or clever man; — an intriguer; — name of the younger brother of Dhrita-rāshṭra and Pāṇḍu.

adj. & s.m. Corrupting, vitiating, defiling, contaminating; — censorious, detracting; — jocular, facetious, witty; — a censorious man, a detractor; — a facetious man, a jester, wag, buffoon; (in the drama) the jocose companion and confidential friend of the principal character or hero of the play; — a libertine; — a catamite.
adj. & s.m. corr. of vidūshak, q.v.
s.m. The act of corrupting or defiling; corruption, contamination; — censuring, reviling, abuse, satire.
s.m. corr. of vidūshan, q.v.
v.t. To corrupt, defile, contaminate; to disgrace; to blame, censure, calumniate, revile, abuse.
s.f. Strength of learning, power of knowledge.
adj. & s.m. Knowing, intelligent, wise, learned, clever; — a wise or learned man, a scholar, a sage, a seer.

adj. = vi-dhan, q.v. s.m. Sending; ordering; arrangement, &c. (i.q. bidhān, q.v.); — form, manner, mode; — act, action; the performance of an act prescribed by religion or law; — means, expedient; — wealth; — fodder (of elephants).
voc. sing. of vidvān (base vid-vat), q.v.
s.m. Enmity, hatred; contempt.
adj. (f. -inī), Hating; hostile, inimical, malicious; — s.m. An enemy.

s.f. Knowledge, learning, scholarship; science, philosophy; — a practical art; — a spell, incantation; — magical skill; — a kind of magical pill or bolus (which placed in the mouth is supposed to give the power of ascending to heaven); — an epithet of the goddess Durgā; — the tree Premna spinosa
subs: (˚yā+abh˚), s.m. Practice or pursuit of learning, study of science; application to books, study
s.m. The study of science; study
s.f. Acquirement of knowledge; — any acquisition made by learning
(base vidyā-dātr̤i), s.m. (f. -trī), One who gives or imparts knowledge, a teacher, an instructor
s.m. The imparting of knowledge, teaching, instruction
s.m. Wealth consisting in learning; money or property acquired by learning or scholarship, or by science, or instruction
(˚yā+ar˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Seeking for or desirous of knowledge, studious; — a student, pupil, scholar, disciple (syn. t̤ālib-ʻilm)
adj. & s.m. Exceedingly learned; the very embodiment of learning
or vidyālay (yā+āl), s.m. Abode or seat of learning, a school, college, seminary
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Passionately fond of learning or study; — a studious person
adj. & s.m. (f. -vatī), Possessed of knowledge or learning, learned; — a learned man, a scholar
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Destitute of knowledge, unlearned, ignorant; — an unlearned or illiterate man.

def: adj. Being perceived or known, being found, being present, being in existence, existent, being; actual, real.
s.f. = S وديمانتو विद्यमानत्व vidyamānatva, s.m. Being, existence, presence.
s.m. = S وديمانتا विद्यमानता vidyamānatā, s.f. Being, existence, presence.
adj. & s.m. corr. of vidyā-vān, q.v.
s.f. Lightning (i.q. bijlī, q.v.)

ety: inf. n. of وَافَقَ 'to agree with,' &c., iii of وَفِقَ; see wafq
def: s.m. Consent, assent; agreement, concord, harmony, good understanding, conformity; unanimity.
s.m. Offensive smell, stink, fœtor.
part. (f. -ā), Gone, gone away, departed; disappeared; ceased; deceased, dead; — parted, separated; — lost; — devoid or destitute (of); — destitute of light, obscure, gloomy, dark.
s.f. Departure, disappearance; separation; — badness; degeneracy, deterioration; — bad conduct, misdemeanour.
s.m. Destruction, killing, murder; — a blow; — opposition; prohibition, prevention; hindrance, impediment, obstacle; interruption; — abandoning.
s.m. Breaking up, destruction, ruin, misfortune.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Killing, destroying; — removing; — opposing, obstructing, impeding, &c.; — destroyer, slayer, murderer; remover; — opposer, &c.
s.m. Obstacle, impediment, hindrance, obstruction, interruption, disturbance, prevention, opposition; detriment; difficulty, trouble, calamity
subs: s.m. 'King of obstacles,' an epithet of Ganeś (who is invoked at the commencement of an undertaking as the remover of obstacles and difficulties)
s.f. The settling or removal of obstacles
or vighn-kārī, or vighn-kar, adj. & s.m. Causing any obstacle or interruption; disturbing, opposing, impeding, hindering; — obstructer, interrupter, disturber, opposer, &c.
adj. & s.m. Who or what removes obstacles or difficulties; — remover of obstacles, &c.; — an epithet of Ganeś
s.m. The destruction or removal of obstacles; — remover of obstacles (i.q. vighn-nāśak), an epithet of Ganeś
or vighn-vidāraṇ, s.m. 'Remover, or tearer away, of obstacles,' epithets of Ganeś (see vighn-rāj)
or vighn-hārī, s.m. 'Remover of obstacles,' or vighneś (˚na+īś˚), s.m. 'Lord of obstacles,' epithets of Ganeś. (For other compounds see bighan.)

def: part. (f. -ā), Obstructed, hindered, inpeded, interrupted, stopped, prevented.

s.m. A stretching out; extension; expansion; diffusion; — shape, form, figure; — the body; — (in Gram.) separation, resolution, analysis; resolution of a compound word into its constituent parts; the proper form of a compound word; — a division, part, portion; opposition, encounter, quarrel, strife, combat, war, battle; — conflict (of the planets); — disfavour.
adj. (f. -ā), Void of qualities; having no distinguishing qualities, void of merit, worthless, bad; imperfect; barren.
part. (f. -ā), Concealed, hidden; scarcely perceptible, indistinct, obscure; — blamed, reproached, censured.
s.m. (f. -ā), A bird; a cloud; an arrow; the sun; the moon; a planet.
v.t. = bihānā, q.v. v.n. To spend or pass time.

ety: S. vihan (fr. vi-ha)+ga, and gama; cf. vihag
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Sky-going, flying, going swiftly; — a bird, &c. (i.q. vihag, q.v.)
subs: or vihaṅg-var, 'King of birds,' or 'best of birds,' epithets of Garuḍ (the vehicle of Vishṇu).

ety: S. vihan (fr. vi-ha)+ga, and gama; cf. vihag
def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Sky-going, flying, going swiftly; — a bird, &c. (i.q. vihag, q.v.)
subs: or vihaṅg-var, 'King of birds,' or 'best of birds,' epithets of Garuḍ (the vehicle of Vishṇu).

def: s.f. = S وهنگکا विहंगिका vihaṅgikā, s.f. A pole or yoke for carrying burdens (i.q. bahaṅgī, q.v.).
s.f. = S وهنگما विहंगमा vihaṅgamā, s.f. A pole or yoke for carrying burdens (i.q. bahaṅgī, q.v.).

s.m. Walking for pleasure or amusement, taking an airing, roving, roaming, wandering; — sporting, play, sport, recreation, &c. (i.q. bihār, q.v.); — a Buddhist or Jain temple or convent; a temple; a palace; — name of a district (see bihār); — the shoulder
subs: s.m: Place of amusement or pleasure; — vihār karnā, v.n. See bihār karnā.

def: s.m. The act of taking away, removing, taking; — going about for pleasure or exercise, taking a walk or airing, rambling, roaming; — relaxation, pastime, pleasure.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Taking pleasure or relaxation, wandering or roaming about for pleasure, enjoying oneself; — sportive, frolicsome, gay; — beautiful, &c. (see bihārī).

ety: Prk. विहरणअं; S. विहरणीयं, rt. विहृ
def: v.n. To enjoy pleasure; to rejoice; — v.t. To take pleasure in, to enjoy; — to rejoice, delight.
v.n. To laugh gently, to smile, to laugh.
s.m. The open air, space, atmosphere, sky, heavens; — a bird.
part. (f. -ā), Left, abandoned, deserted; desolate. lonely

part. (f. -ā), Distributed, apportioned; — put in order, arranged; appointed, determined, established, instituted, fixed, settled, prescribed, enjoined, enacted, decreed, ordained; proper (to be done), fit.

ety: Prk. विहूणिओ or विहीणिओ; S. विहीन+इकः
def: adj. Foreign, strange (see vihīn; and cf. g̠arīb).

part. adj. (f. -ā), Shaking about, agitated, alarmed, perturbed, disquieted, confused, overcome with fear (or grief, or passion), beside oneself; infatuated; delirious; distressed, afflicted; — languishing; desponding; — fused, liquid.
s.f. = S وهولتو विव्हलत्व vi-hvalatva, s.m. Agitation, perturbation, anxiety, commotion, consternation.
s.m. = S وهولتا विव्हलता vi-hvalatā, s.f. Agitation, perturbation, anxiety, commotion, consternation.
s.m. Seed, &c. (i.q. bīj, q.v.)
subs: (˚ja+ak˚), s.m. The first syllable of a magical formula, the syllable at the commencement of a mantra or form of prayer
(˚ja+an˚), s.m. Sprout or first shoot from a seed, seed-shoot, cotyledon, seedling
s.m. Seed vessel, pericarp (of a flower)
s.m. Calculation of primary causes; analysis; algebra; — name of a treatise on algebra
(or bīj-mār), adj. 'Killing the seed'; epithet of a soil that returns not produce equal to the seed sown; — s.m. Failure of germination. (For other comp. see bīj.)

def: s.m. See s.v. vīj. s.m. Seed, &c. (i.q. bīj, q.v.)
subs: (˚ja+ak˚), s.m. The first syllable of a magical formula, the syllable at the commencement of a mantra or form of prayer
(˚ja+an˚), s.m. Sprout or first shoot from a seed, seed-shoot, cotyledon, seedling
s.m. Seed vessel, pericarp (of a flower)
s.m. Calculation of primary causes; analysis; algebra; — name of a treatise on algebra
(or bīj-mār), adj. 'Killing the seed'; epithet of a soil that returns not produce equal to the seed sown; — s.m. Failure of germination. (For other comp. see bīj.)

def: adj. Without people or inhabitants, uninhabited, unfrequented; solitary, desolate, lonely; private.
s.m. Fanning, the being fanned; — a fan (i.q. vyajan); — a thing, substance; — a sort of pheasant; — the ruddy goose.

ety: vījan prob. dialec. plur. of vīj
def: adj. & s.m. = vīj-mār, q.v.s.v. vīj.
s.f. Different origin or birth; different species; different caste, or tribe, or kind.

adj. (f. -ā), Of different or mixed origin; of different birth or caste; of another (or a different) tribe or species; of a different kind or sort; dissimilar; — uncommon; — of a bad description; base-born; bastard; — s.m. A base-born person, a bastard, &c.

s.m. Overcoming, defeating; surpassing, excelling; — conquest, victory, triumph (i.q. bijaʼe, q.v.); — a name of Arjun; — one of the two gate-keepers of the Hindū paradise; — name of a particular auspicious hour or period
subs: or vijay-mant, adj. (f. -matī), Possessing victory, victorious, triumphant (i.q. vijayī).

def: s.f. Hemp, &c. (i.q. bijayā, q.v.); — name of various plants; — a name of the goddess Durgā; — name of a festival in honour of Durgā (on the tenth day of the light half of the month Aśvin, when the image of Durgā is cast into the water).
s.m. 'The victorious,' an epithet of Indra.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Conquering, victorious, triumphant; — a conqueror.
adj. Mixed with the water of boiled rice, or with gruel (as sauce, &c.).
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī) = bījī, q.v. s.f. Weasel; mongoose, Viverra Ichneumon (syn. neʼolā).
adj. = vijayin, q.v. adj. Mixed with the water of boiled rice, or with gruel (as sauce, &c.).
adj. = vijayin, q.v. adj. Mixed with the water of boiled rice, or with gruel (as sauce, &c.).
part. (f. -ā), Conquered, overcome, subdued, defeated, won, gained
subs: (˚ta+in˚), adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having the organs of sense (or the passions) subdued; — one whose passions are under subjection.

def: adj. = vijayin, q.v. adj. Mixed with the water of boiled rice, or with gruel (as sauce, &c.).
adj. (f. -ā), Knowing, intelligent; wise, learned; proficient, conversant, clever, experienced, skilful, prudent, discreet; — s.m. A wise man; a scholar, &c.

s.m. Distinguishing, perceiving, recognizing, discerning, understanding; distinction, perception, discernment, intelligence, knowledge, comprehension; science, learning, worldly knowledge of any kind (including all subjects except that understanding of the true nature of God or Brahma which is acquirable only by abstract meditation and the study of the Vedas); wisdom; — business, employment; — music
subs: s.m. The removal or destruction of learning, &c.

def: s.f. Learnedness; learning, intelligence (see vigyān).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Who or what makes known or apprizes, making known, giving information; — an informant; — an instructor.
s.m. The act of making known, or acquainting, or teaching; informing, representing; information; instruction; announcement, respectful communication or representation (cf. nivedan).
s.f. Information, instruction; announcement, &c. (i.q. vi-gyāpan).
s.f. Information, representation, respectful statement or communication; a report, an announcement.
part. (f. -ā), Discerned, understood, perceived, known; — celebrated, famous, notorious (syn. mashhūr).
s.m. One who knows or understands, an experienced person; a witness.
s.f. = S وجنتو विज्ञत्व vi-jṅatva, vulg. vigyatva, s.m. Intelligence, wisdom, learning, experience, readiness, skill, cleverness.
s.f. 1˚ corr. of vijṅapti; — 2˚ of vi-jṅatā.
s.m. = S وجنتا विज्ञता vi-jṅatā, vulg. vigyatā, s.f. Intelligence, wisdom, learning, experience, readiness, skill, cleverness.
part. adj. Having known or perceived, having understood, having ascertained, having recognized.
adj. (f. -ā), Free from fever, or from pain; free from distress; — exempt from decay.

ety: inf. n. of وقع 'to fall upon, to attack,' &c.
def: s.m. Falling or rushing (upon); attack (in battle), onset; engagement; — copulation.
s.m. Afternoon, close of day, twilight, evening, dusk.

adj. (f. -ā), Deprived of a part, or limb, or member; not complete; defective, imperfect; wanting, failing; — out of order, impaired; decayed; maimed; withered; on the wane; — confused, confounded, disconcerted; agitated, distressed, discomposed, restless, uneasy, anxious, troubled, sorrowful; — s.m. The sixtieth part of a kalā or of a degree, a second.
s.f. An anxious (or a sorrowful, &c.) woman; — a woman in whom menstruation has ceased; — the sixtieth part of a degree, a second.
s.m. = vikāl.
s.f. = S وکلتو विकलत्व vi-kalatva, s.m. Defectiveness, imperfection; impaired state or condition; — irregularity; disorder; confusion, agitation, anxiety, restlessness, uneasiness, distress.
s.m. = S وکلتا विकलता vi-kalatā, s.f. Defectiveness, imperfection; impaired state or condition; — irregularity; disorder; confusion, agitation, anxiety, restlessness, uneasiness, distress.
s.f. A woman in whom menstruation has ceased.
s.f. A perforated copper vessel which (when placed in a pan of water) marks the time by gradually filling; a sort of clepsydra or water-clock.

s.m. Alternation, alternative, option; — distinction; uncertainty, ambiguity; indecision, doubt, hesitation; — error, mistake, ignorance; — (in Gram.) admission of an option or alternative, allowing a rule to be observed or not, at pleasure; — (in Rhet.) antithesis.
adj. (f. -ā), = S وکمپت विकम्पित vi-kampit, part. (f. -ā), Trembling, tremulous, quivering; heaving, palpitating; agitated; unsteady.
part. (f. -ā), = S وکمپ विकम्प vi-kamp, adj. (f. -ā), Trembling, tremulous, quivering; heaving, palpitating; agitated; unsteady.
s.m. A kind of herbaceous plant, Hedysarum alhagi, or Alhagi maurorum (lit. 'having no thorns').
s.m. See the pop. form بکار bikār.
adj. (f. -ā), See the pop. form بکرال bikrāl.
s.m. Various business or duty; — prohibited or unlawful act, misdeed, fraud; — retiring from business; cessation from labour
subs: s.f. An illegal act; an immoral act.

def: s.m. (lit. 'having large ears'), Name of one of the Kuru princes on the side of Duryodhan, in the war against the Pāṇḍavas.
s.m. The district called Sārasvat, in the north-west of Hindūstān (generally considered to be a part of the Panjāb, and said to be so called as being 'the region from which Karṇ was excluded').
s.m. See vi-kāś, and bikās.

s.m. Becoming visible, appearance, display, manifestation, exhibition; — open or splendid appearance; — expanse, sky, heaven, ether; — a shining, blooming; — opening, expanding, expansion; budding, blowing (as a flower, or the heart; but in these senses better vikās); — pleasure, joy, happiness, enjoyment; — absence of manifestation or display; solitude, loneliness, privacy.
perf. part. of wikasnā, q.v. (and i.q. vikasit, q.v.). part. See vi-kāśit, and bikāsit.
adj. (f. -ikā), Causing to appear or shine forth, displaying; illustrating; illuminating; — causing to expand or bloom, opening, unfolding, spreading (i.q. vikāsak).
s.m. The act of displaying, display, manifestation, exhibition; — expansion, blowing (of a flower; — in this sense better vikāsan).
s.f. Appearance, display, manifestation, publicity; — expansion, diffusion.
adj. (f. -inī), Becoming visible or apparent, becoming manifested, shining forth; — expanding, spreading, developing, opening, budding, blowing (in these senses better vikāsī).
part. See vi-kāśit, and bikāsit.
part. (f. -ā), Made visible, displayed, exhibited; illumined; resplendent; radiant; — expanded, spread out, opened, blown, budded (in these senses better vikāsit).
part. (f. -ā), Expanded, opened, unfolded, budded, blown (as a flower); — rejoiced, cheered, delighted.
part. corr. of vikasit, q.v. part. See vi-kāśit, and bikāsit.
v.n. = بکسنا bikasnā, q.v. v.n. To open, expand; to blow (as a flower), bloom; to be pleased or delighted, become radiant with joy; to smile, laugh
subs: v.n. To be blighted, to fade, droop; to be displeased.

def: adj. Large, great, &c. (i.q. bikaṭ, q.v.); — changed in form or appearance; distorted; — beautiful, pleasing; — obscure; — obsolete; — s.m. (S. vikaṭam), Danger, jeopardy; fright, terror, awe; — a boil, tumour.
s.m. Making known, declaring, announcing, publishing; — explaining, expounding; explanation, exposition; — avowing, acknowledging, confessing.
part. (f. -ā), Generally known, notorious, famous, celebrated, glorious; victorious (syn. mashhūr); known as, nominated, named, called; — avowed, confessed.
s.f. Fame, renown, celebrity, notoriety (syn. shuhrat).

s.m. Stepping or going beyond, striding over or along; proceeding, going; — a step, stride, pace; — overcoming, overpowering; — great power or strength; — prowess, heroic valour, heroism; — name of a king (i.q. vikramāditya)
subs: (˚ma+ād˚), vulg. vikram-ājīt, or bikramājīt, s.m. Name of a celebrated Hindū king (of Ujjayinī or Oujein, and founder of the era called Samvat (still in use among the Hindūs) which begins 57 B.C.
(˚ma+ar˚), s.m. A name of king Vikram
s.m. 'The city of Vikram,' an epithet of Ujjain
or vikram-sen, s.m. A name of king Vikram.

def: adj. & s.m. Displaying valour or prowess; powerful, strong, mighty; courageous, valiant, heroic; — a hero; — a lion.

part. (f. -ā), Stepped or passed beyond; — overcoming; victorious; pre-eminent; courageous, valiant, mighty; — s.m. A hero; warrior; — a lion; — (S. vi-krāntam), s.m. A step, stride; gait; — valour, prowess; military achievement.
s.f. Stepping on or out, striding; — canter, gallop (of a horse); — power, great strength; valour, prowess, heroism.
s.m. Selling, vending; sale; demand.
s.m. (f. -inī) = S وکريك विक्रयिक vi-krayik, s.m. (f. -ā) = vi-kretā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -ā) = S وکريي विक्रयी vi-krayī, s.m. (f. -inī) = vi-kretā, q.v.
s.m. (f. -trī), One who sells, seller, vendor, salesman, dealer.

part. (f. -ā), Altered, changed; modified; transformed; — changed in feeling, estranged (from); — entertaining feelings of disgust or aversion, averse (to); — changed for the worse, impaired; sick, diseased; mutilated, maimed, deformed; — unnatural; — imperfect, incomplete; strange, extraordinary; — (S. vi-kr̤itam), s.m. Alteration, change; change for the worse; — impaired or diseased state; — estrangement; — aversion, disgust
subs: (˚ta+āk˚), or vikr̤itākr̤iti (˚ta+āk˚), adj. Changed in form or appearance, transformed; — having a deformed shape or aspect, misshaped, distorted in form
(˚ta+ān˚), or vikr̤it-mukh, or vikr̤it-vadan, adj. Having an altered or a distorted face; ugly-faced; — s.m. A person with an altered face, &c.

def: part. (f. -ā), Sold.
adj. See s.v. vikr̤it.
adj. See s.v. vikr̤it.

s.f. Alteration, or change of any kind (as of purpose, mind, form, nature, &c.); change from a natural or healthy state, sickness, disease; — change from a quiescent state, emotion, passion, anger; fear, apprehension.

s.m. Throwing, casting, hurling, discharging; — projection; — throwing away; throwing about, scattering; sending, despatching; — dispersion, dissipation; — refutation (of an argument); — confusion, agitation, perplexity; craziness; alarm, fear; — (in Astron.) celestial latitude.
s.m. Throwing, &c. (i.q. vikshep).
part. (f. -ā), Thrown, cast; thrown about, scattered, dispersed; — sent, despatched; — refuted, falsified; agitated, bewildered, perplexed; crazed, demented, foolish.
s.m. A hole; a burrow (see bil); a chasm, fissure, vacuity; a cave, cavern, den; — (S. विलः) one of Indra's horses.
s.f. Time, season, juncture; — thing, business, matter
subs: At this time, at the present time; now-a-days; — in this matter.

ety: inf. n. of وَلَدَ 'to beget children,' &c.
def: s.m. Procreation, generation; — nativity; — child-birth (i.q. wilādat).

ety: for A. ولادة, inf. n. of ولد 'to bear children'
def: s.f. Birth, nativity.
adj. & s.m. Separate, detached, apart, &c. (i.q. bilag, q.v.). adj. Separate, detached; — s.m. Separation, disunion, difference
subs: v.n. To be displeased, offended, or angry.

def: adj. (f. -ā), Shameless, unabashed; immodest, impudent (i.q. nilajj, q.v.).
adj. (f. -ā), Having a character different from what is usual or natural, strange, extraordinary; — bewildered, embarrassed; astonished, surprised; — abashed, ashamed.

adj. (f. -ā), Having different or opposite marks or characteristics; having distinguishing qualities or features; different, other, peculiar, strange, extraordinary; good; — s.m. A state or condition for which no cause can be assigned; vain or causeless state; — the act of distinguishing, perceiving, seeing, observing.
s.m. A cat (i.q. viḍāl, q.v.). s.m. A cat (i.q. bilār); — the eye-ball.
s.m. Hanging, falling down, depending; pendulousness; — slowness, tardiness, delay, prolonged time, long stay; procrastination (i.q. bilamb, q.v. for phrases).
s.m. Hanging down, depending; — delaying; delay, procrastination.
v.t. = bilmānā, q.v. v.t. To keep back, delay, detain, withhold.
s.m. Giving; gift, donation; liberality.

s.m. corr. of vilamb, q.v. (cf. bilambh). s.m. Hanging, falling down, depending; pendulousness; — slowness, tardiness, delay, prolonged time, long stay; procrastination (i.q. bilamb, q.v. for phrases).
part. (f. -ā), Hanging down, pendulous; — hanging or suspended (from), attached (to), closely connected (with); — delayed, retarded, procrastinated; slow, tardy; slow (time, in Music).
v.n. = bilambnā, q.v. v.n. To stay, tarry, delay, &c. ( = bilamb rahnā; bilamb karnā); to procrastinate.
s.m. Weeping, wailing, lamentation, moaning, complaint, the language of grief and distress (i.q. bilāp); moan, wail, groan
subs: To weep, wail, cry aloud, lament, make complaint, &c.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā) = vilāpī, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Weeping, wailing, lamenting, complaining; — one who weeps or wails, bewailer, lamenter, &c.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Weeping, wailing, lamenting, complaining; — one who weeps or wails, bewailer, lamenter, &c.
v.n. To weep, wail, &c. (i.q. vilāp karnā).

s.m. Play, sport, merriment, pastime, pleasure, delight, enjoyment (com. bilās); amorous pastime, diversion (with women, dancers, singers, &c.), dalliance, coquetry, affectation of coyness, wantonness; — grace, elegance, charm, beauty
subs: To sport, play, enjoy oneself, take pleasure, &c.

def: s.m. Flashing, gleaming; — sporting; — dallying; — play, sport, mirth.

adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Playful, sportive, wanton, dallying, amorous; — addicted to pleasure, sensual, voluptuous; — a sporter, &c.; one who is fond of pleasure; a voluptuary, sensualist, rake; — fire; — the moon; — a snake; — an epithet of Śiva; of Vishṇu; of Krishṇ; and of Kām-dev.
s.f. (of next), A woman of pleasure, a coquettish woman; a wanton woman, a harlot; — a woman.
v.n. = بلسنا bilasnā, q.v. v.n. To be pleased or delighted; to derive pleasure (from), take delight (in), to enjoy (oneself).

v.n. = vilās karnā, q.v. s.m. Play, sport, merriment, pastime, pleasure, delight, enjoyment (com. bilās); amorous pastime, diversion (with women, dancers, singers, &c.), dalliance, coquetry, affectation of coyness, wantonness; — grace, elegance, charm, beauty
subs: To sport, play, enjoy oneself, take pleasure, &c.

def: s.m. Liquefaction; dissolution, destruction, death; destruction of the world.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian ولی (walī), from Arabic وَلِيّ (waliyy).

def: s.m. The act of dissolving, or liquefying; dissolution, liquefaction; destroying, destruction; corroding, eating away; removing, taking away; attenuating; (in Med.) an attenuant, escharotic.

adj. European, foreign; English; Persian; Turkish; Chinese, &c.; — of English manufacture, English-made; — s.m. A foreigner; — a native of Afghānistān; — an article of foreign manufacture; — a simpleton, a fool
subs: s.m. The tomato or love-apple, Solanum lycopersicum
s.m. A kind of Persian nastaʻlīq hand
s.m. The Cape gooseberry, or Brazil cherry, Physalis peruviana.

def: s.m. Attraction, allurement, temptation, seduction, delusion.
s.m. Leading astray, perplexing, beguiling; attracting, alluring, tempting, seducing; attraction, &c. (i.q. vilobh); — praise, commendation, flattery.
s.m. The eye; sight
subs: s.m. A cast or glance of the eye, a glance, look.

def: s.m. Agitating, shaking, stirring; whirling round; churning; rolling; tossing.
s.m. The act of looking, seeing, &c. (see bilokan).
part. adj. (f. -ā), To be seen or looked at; worthy of being looked at; agreeable, beautiful.
part. (f. -ā), Looked at, viewed, beheld, seen, observed, regarded, examined, contemplated.

ety: wilok˚ = S. विलोकय(ति), caus. of वि+लोक्
def: v.t. = biloknā, q.v. v.t. To look at, look on or upon, behold, regard, view, observe, consider, examine, inspect; to perceive, see, spy, discern.
s.m. = vi-loḍan, q.v. s.m. Agitating, shaking, stirring; whirling round; churning; rolling; tossing.
adj. Against the hair; against the grain; out of the usual or proper course; turned the wrong way, turned backward, reverse, inverted, inverse, contrary, opposite
subs: s.m. The rule of three inverse
s.f., or vilom-vidhi, s.m. Reverse action, doing anything in reverse order or backwards; — (in Arith.) the rule of inversion.

ety: wiloṛ˚ = S. विलोडय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+लुड्
def: v.t. = biloṛnā, q.v. (and cf. viloḍan). v.t. To stir, shake, agitate, to churn.
s.m. A sort of fish, Clupsa cultrata.
v.t. = wiloṛnā, or biloṛnā, q.v. v.t. = biloṛnā, q.v. (and cf. viloḍan). v.t. To stir, shake, agitate, to churn.
s.m. The wood-apple tree, and its fruit, Ӕgle marmelos (com. called bel, q.v.).
s.m. Name of a monkey-demon (slain by Balarām).
adj. (f. -ā), Free from dirt or impurity, pure, clean, spotless, stainless; undefiled, uncontaminated, immaculate; — white; transparent; beautiful
subs: or vimalātmā (˚la+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Pure-minded, clean, pure, stainless, unpolluted; — a pure-minded person, &c.
(˚la = ar˚), adj. Of pure purpose; pure-minded, &c. (i.q. vimalātmak)

def: adj. See s.v. vimal. adj. (f. -ā), Free from dirt or impurity, pure, clean, spotless, stainless; undefiled, uncontaminated, immaculate; — white; transparent; beautiful
subs: or vimalātmā (˚la+āt˚), adj. & s.m. Pure-minded, clean, pure, stainless, unpolluted; — a pure-minded person, &c.
(˚la = ar˚), adj. Of pure purpose; pure-minded, &c. (i.q. vimalātmak)

def: s.m. A measure; — a car or chariot of the gods, &c. (i.q. bimān, q.v.; — syn. taḵẖt-ě-ravān); any car or vehicle; a horse; — a palace.
s.m. A by-road; a bad road; a wrong road (lit. or fig.), evil conduct, vicious course, immorality (i.q. vi-path, q.v.); — a broom, brush.
s.m. Examination by reason; reasoning, discussion; consideration, deliberation; investigation, trial; — hesitation, doubt; — (prop. vi-marsh) dissatisfaction, displeasure; dislike; regret.
part. (f. -ā), Disagreed, disagreeing; of a different mind or disposition; at variance; discordant, discrepant; opposed, or hostile (to), averse (to), disapproving; — displeased, offended.
s.f. A stepmother, a father's wife.
s.f. Difference of mind or opinion; disagreement, dissent; discrepancy, discordance; dislike, aversion.
s.f. A stepmother, a father's wife.
s.m. = بمب bimb, q.v. s.m. Disc of the sun or moon; reflected form, reflection, shadow, image, picture; a plant bearing a bright-red gourd, Momordica monadelpha; the fruit of this plant.
s.f. = بمنا bimbā, q.v. s.f. The plant Momordica monadelpha ( = bimb).
s.m. Disc (of the sun or moon); — a plant bearing a bright red gourd, Momordica monadelpha; the fruit of that plant (i.q. vimb).
s.f. = vimbā = bimbā, q.v. s.f. = بمنا bimbā, q.v. s.f. The plant Momordica monadelpha ( = bimb).
s.f. = vimbak, q.v. s.m. Disc (of the sun or moon); — a plant bearing a bright red gourd, Momordica monadelpha; the fruit of that plant (i.q. vimb).
part. (f. -ā), Imaged, reflected, shadowed out or forth, pictured, painted.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā, or -ī), Having lips like the vimb-fruit, red-lipped, 'cherry-lipped'; — a person with beautifully red lips.
adj. = بمبك bimbuk, q.v. adj. Red, fiery, flame-coloured.
part. (f. -ā), Unfaded; free from decay; refreshed, revived; fresh; — unsoiled; pure, clean.
s.m. The act of unloosing, unharnessing, unyoking; setting free, liberating, releasing; release, liberation, emancipation, salvation (i.q. vi-moksh).
part. (f. -ā), Loosened, set free, liberated, released, emancipated; — forgiven, pardoned.
s.m. Bewilderment; infatuation; temptation, allurement, seduction.
s.m. The act of confounding the mind and exciting the passions, tempting, alluring, seducing; temptation, seduction; infatuation, bewilderment.
adj. & s.m. Tempting, alluring, seductive, bewitching, fascinating; — a tempter, seducer, &c.
part. (f. -ā), Allured, beguiled, tempted, seduced; infatuated, bewitched; bereft of consciousness, bewildered, confounded, stupefied, besotted.

s.m. = S وموکشن विमोक्षण vi-mokshaṇ, s.m. = S وموکشنا विमोक्षणा vi-mokshaṇā, s.f. Untying, letting loose, setting free; release, liberation, freedom; final emancipation; forgiveness; letting off; dismissing; quitting, abandoning; resigning; discharging; depositing (of. vimoćan).

s.m. = S وموکش विमोक्ष vi-moksh, s.m. = S وموکشنا विमोक्षणा vi-mokshaṇā, s.f. Untying, letting loose, setting free; release, liberation, freedom; final emancipation; forgiveness; letting off; dismissing; quitting, abandoning; resigning; discharging; depositing (of. vimoćan).

s.f. = S وموکش विमोक्ष vi-moksh, s.m. = S وموکشن विमोक्षण vi-mokshaṇ, s.m. Untying, letting loose, setting free; release, liberation, freedom; final emancipation; forgiveness; letting off; dismissing; quitting, abandoning; resigning; discharging; depositing (of. vimoćan).
adj. (f. -ī), Having the face turned away or cast down, with averted face; averse (to), disinclined, opposed (to), hostile.
s.f. The state of having the face averted; turning away; — disinclination; — opposition; — disappearance, departure.
adj. = vi-mukh, q.v. adj. (f. -ī), Having the face turned away or cast down, with averted face; averse (to), disinclined, opposed (to), hostile.
part. (f. -ā), Loosed, let loose, set free, released, liberated; emancipated; freed (from), relieved (of); dismissed; — parted, separated.
s.f. Liberation, release; remission; escape, salvation, final emancipation (from all future existence); — separation, parting.
part. (f. -ā), Confounded, bewildered; beguiled, infatuated, led astray, tempted, seduced; foolish, stupid
subs: (˚ḍha+āt˚), adj. Foolish-minded, senseless; beguiled, &c. (i.q. vimūṛh); — s.m. An infatuated person; a foolish person.

def: pron. (Braj) = un, the pl. format. of the pron. wah (cf. wis).
s.f. contrac. of next, q.v. (com. bīn, q.v.).
prep. (or prefix) & postpn. = bin or binā, qq.v. v.n. To die a premature, or a sudden, death (see āʼī)
subs: adj. Unsown, uncultivated, wild, spontaneous ( = an-boyā; ḵẖud-rau)
adj. & s.m. Toothless; — a toothless person
adj. Without meat and drink, without eating and drinking
adv. Without meditation, without reflection, unthinkingly, inconsiderately
adv. Uselessly, fruitlessly, to no purpose
v.n. To fear without a cause; to cry out before being hurt
bin teg̠ shahīd hona, v.n. To be slain without a wound (said of lovers).

ety: prob. Ap. Prk. वीणअं; G. वैणि+कं
def: s.m. A bundle of thread, a skein.
adj. & s.m. corr. of P. بينا bīnā, q.v.
prep. (or prefix) & postpn. = bin or binā, qq.v. v.n. To die a premature, or a sudden, death (see āʼī)
subs: adj. Unsown, uncultivated, wild, spontaneous ( = an-boyā; ḵẖud-rau)
adj. & s.m. Toothless; — a toothless person
adj. Without meat and drink, without eating and drinking
adv. Without meditation, without reflection, unthinkingly, inconsiderately
adv. Uselessly, fruitlessly, to no purpose
v.n. To fear without a cause; to cry out before being hurt
bin teg̠ shahīd hona, v.n. To be slain without a wound (said of lovers).
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of بِن (bin) and بِنَا (binā), a tadbhava.

def: s.f. The Indian lute (i.q. bīn or bīnā, qq.v.; — the Vīṇā is supposed to have been invented by Nārad, the son of Brahmā, and has many varieties, which are enumerated according to the number of strings, &c.; its compass is said to be two octaves)
subs: s.m. Name of one of the Gandharvs.

def: s.m. corr. of وناش vi-nāś, q.v.
s.m. Disappearance; destruction, &c. (i.q. binās, q.v.)
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Destroying, ruining; destructive (of), ruinous; annihilating; — a destroyer, ruiner, spoiler, &c.
adj. & s.m. = vi-nāśak, q.v.; — s.m. = vi-nāś, q.v. adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Destroying, ruining; destructive (of), ruinous; annihilating; — a destroyer, ruiner, spoiler, &c.

s.m. Loss; disappearance; perishing; — destroying, destruction; — name of the place where the river Sarasvatī is lost in the sand; — name of a country in the north-west of Delhi (said to be the same as Kuru-kshetr, or the country adjacent to the modern Paniput).
v.t. To cause to deteriorate, &c.; to destroy, ruin, spoil.
part. (f. -ā), Utterly lost; disappeared; perished; — destroyed, ruined; spoilt, corrupted.
s.f. Utter loss; disappearance; destruction, ruin.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Being destroyed, perishing; undergoing change or transformation; — destroying, destructive; — perishable, destructible; — a destroyer, &c. (i.q. vināśak).
